data.title: Emerging Alternatives to U.S. and Chinese AI Models
data.description: While complete independence from U.S. or Chinese AI models remains unlikely in the near future, new alternatives are beginning to surface. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing interest in developing AI technologies that do not rely solely on these dominant models. This shift is driven by the desire for more diverse and localized AI solutions that can cater to specific regional needs and regulations. As the global AI landscape evolves, countries are exploring ways to foster innovation and reduce dependency on major players, paving the way for a more varied and competitive market.

data.title: Investment Strategies: Avoiding Poor-Performing Companies
data.description:
Investors often seek strategies to maximize returns by steering clear of underperforming companies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the importance of identifying and avoiding investments in companies with poor financial health or negative market outlooks. This approach can be crucial for maintaining a robust investment portfolio.

Financial experts suggest that thorough research and analysis are essential in recognizing companies that may not yield favorable returns. By examining financial statements, market trends, and industry performance, investors can make informed decisions to avoid potential losses.

The strategy of avoiding poorly performing companies is not just about minimizing risk but also about reallocating resources to more promising opportunities. This method can lead to more stable and potentially profitable investment outcomes.

In conclusion, while the market offers numerous investment opportunities, discerning investors prioritize due diligence to ensure their portfolios are not burdened by companies with bleak prospects.

data.description: 1. Vitalik responds to Ethereum's "slow death" rhetoric: plans four major system upgrades; 2. data.title: ECB Official Highlights Tariff Impact on U.S. Economy
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member and Bank of Italy Governor Fabio Panetta has commented on the economic impact of tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump. According to Jin10, Panetta stated that the U.S. economy has borne the majority of the burden from these tariffs. Speaking in Venice on Saturday, he noted that foreign exporters have absorbed approximately 10% of the losses. Initially, U.S. businesses absorbed these losses through reduced profits, but over time, some of the costs have been passed on to consumers, who now bear about half of the burden. Overall, tariffs are estimated to have contributed slightly more than 0.5 percentage points to U.S. inflation.

data.title: BTC/Gold Market Cycle Analysis: Potential Bottom After 14 Months
data.description:
Crypto KOL Ted posted on X about the historical patterns observed in the BTC/Gold market cycle. According to Ted, the BTC/Gold ratio has shown a consistent pattern of bottoming out after 14 months in previous cycles. This trend was noted in 2014, 2018, and 2022, where each cycle reached a bottom after a 14-month period.

Currently, the BTC/Gold bear market has persisted for 14 months, leading to speculation about whether a bottom has been reached in this cycle as well. Ted's analysis suggests that if historical patterns hold, the market may be at or near a bottom, although this remains speculative and subject to market conditions.

The observation raises questions about the predictability of market cycles and whether past performance can be an indicator of future trends. Investors and analysts are closely monitoring the situation to determine if the BTC/Gold ratio will follow the same pattern as in previous years.

data.title: Bitcoin's Resilience Amid Market Volatility and Miner Supply Tightening
data.description:
VanEck released a mid-February 2026 Bitcoin analysis report, highlighting significant market sentiment shifts and leverage adjustments. According to PANews, the report indicates that despite these fluctuations, Bitcoin's on-chain activity remains resilient, with a slowdown in mid-cycle distribution and tightening miner supply suggesting stronger fundamentals than current prices reflect.

Bitcoin has experienced a 29% decline over the past 30 days, leading to deteriorating market sentiment. This downturn has pushed the Net Unrealized Profit/Loss (NUPL) into the anxiety zone, with moments of panic, while leverage reset has brought open interest levels back to those seen in September 2024.

Mid-term holders are driving distribution, although sales have slowed. Actual sales are still concentrated among those holding for 1 to 5 years, with a noticeable slowdown in the distribution of tokens held for over a year in the past month.

Miner profit margins have narrowed, and hash rate has contracted by approximately 14% over the last 90 days, indicating a tightening mining economy. Historically, such conditions have been precursors to stronger future Bitcoin returns.

data.title: Circle's Stock Shows Bullish Signals Amid Strategic Partnerships
data.description:
Chain analysis platform 10x Research recently highlighted the varied performance of crypto-related stocks. According to PANews, Circle's stock (CRCL) has surpassed its 7-day and 30-day moving averages, indicating bullish technical signals with a weekly increase of approximately 3.6%.

On the fundamental side, Circle has entered a strategic partnership with Polymarket, which will use native USDC as its primary settlement asset, expanding USDC's role in prediction markets. Institutional interest is evident as ARK Invest, led by Cathie Wood, increased its holdings by 218,000 shares of CRCL during a market pullback, valued at around $13 million, reflecting growing institutional confidence.

Analysts suggest that under the GENIUS Act framework, the digital dollar reserve may more directly flow into the U.S. Treasury market, positioning Circle as a potential major beneficiary of this trend.

data.title: Coinbase CEO: Coinbase holds over 80% of all BTC and ETH ETF assets in the US, with peak inflows reaching $31 billion in 2025.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated in an article on the X platform that Coinbase hosts over 80% of the BTC and ETH ETF assets in the US and achieved a peak ETF inflow of $31 billion in 2025. Coinbase currently partners with numerous ETF providers, including large financial institutions and governments. Coinbase regularly conducts penetration tests with top cryptographers, holds numerous related technology patents, and continuously performs security audits.

data.description:
The CLARITY Act proposes a stablecoin yield ban by ending automatic interest accrual; Coinbase, Circle and ABA cite risks to reward rules and deposit flows.

data.title: Macron Calls for Calm Ahead of Far-Right March in Lyon
data.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has called for calm in anticipation of a march in Lyon organized by far-right groups. Bloomberg posted on X that the march is intended to honor a man who was beaten to death in a street fight. Macron's appeal comes amid concerns about potential unrest during the event. Authorities are on high alert to ensure public safety and prevent any escalation of violence. The situation remains tense as the march approaches, with officials urging participants to maintain peace.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations may lead to substantial liquidation events on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum's price falls below $1,874, the cumulative liquidation intensity for long positions across mainstream centralized exchanges could reach $860 million. Conversely, if Ethereum surpasses $2,057, the cumulative liquidation intensity for short positions could amount to $732 million.

data.title: Shift in Commodity Markets: From Free Trade to Barriers and Hoarding
data.description:
The global commodity markets are experiencing a significant transformation, moving away from the era of free trade towards a landscape characterized by barriers and hoarding. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the changing dynamics in these markets. This shift is driven by various geopolitical and economic factors, leading countries to prioritize their own resources and implement protective measures.

The trend of hoarding and imposing trade barriers is becoming increasingly prevalent as nations seek to secure their supply chains and mitigate risks associated with global uncertainties. This approach contrasts sharply with the previous emphasis on open markets and unrestricted trade, which facilitated the flow of commodities across borders.

Experts suggest that this change could have profound implications for global trade, potentially leading to increased volatility and price fluctuations in commodity markets. As countries focus on self-sufficiency, the traditional principles of free trade are being challenged, prompting a reevaluation of international trade policies.

The impact of these developments is being felt across various sectors, including agriculture, energy, and metals, as nations adapt to the new realities of commodity trading. The shift towards protectionism and resource hoarding underscores the need for strategic planning and collaboration to navigate the evolving landscape of global trade.

data.title: IoTeX: We have noticed suspicious activity in the token vault and are tracking and freezing the hacker's assets.
data.description: IoTeX issued a statement on the X platform stating that it has taken note of recent reports of suspicious activity regarding the IoTeX token vault. The team is assessing and controlling the situation. Preliminary estimates indicate that the potential losses are far lower than the rumored amount. IoTeX has coordinated with major exchanges and security partners to actively assist in tracing and freezing the hacker's assets. The situation is currently under control, and IoTeX will continue to monitor the situation and update the community on its progress.
